featureName: AI Add-on Billing — Single Subscription Model
description: Unify AI add-on and main subscription into a single Stripe subscription with 1-2 items. Supports AI during trial (billing_cycle_anchor), auto-conversion at trial end, prorated add/remove, annual AI prices.

userStories.title: Stripe price config restructure + helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STRIPE_AI_PRICES nested by interval and isAiPrice()/getTierFromPriceId() helpers so that multi-item subscription logic can reliably identify AI vs main items.
acceptanceCriteria: STRIPE_AI_PRICES is nested: { solo: { month, year }, equipe: { month, year }, agence: { month, year } }
AI_PRICE_IDS Set contains all 6 AI price IDs (3 monthly + 3 annual)
isAiPrice(priceId) returns true for AI prices, false otherwise
getTierFromPriceId(priceId) returns AiTier or null
Existing imports (STRIPE_AI_PRICES, AiTier) still work across codebase
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Added BillingInterval type, isMainPrice(), getIntervalFromAiPriceId() as bonus helpers. Consumers updated: ai-subscription-actions uses .month, webhook uses getTierFromPriceId.

userStories.title: Refactor items.data[0] → find-by-price across billing code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Stripe subscription item access to use isAiPrice() lookup instead of items.data[0] so that multi-item subscriptions work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: subscription.service.ts: upgradeSubscriptionDirect() finds main item via !isAiPrice()
subscription.service.ts: previewUpgrade() finds main item via !isAiPrice()
subscription.service.ts: syncPeriodDatesFromStripe() reads period from main item
subscription-actions.ts: verifyCheckoutSession() finds main item by price
stripe-webhook.handler.ts: all handlers find items by price, not index
subscriptions.stripe_price_id always stores main price ID (not AI)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: HIGH #7 done. 5 locations refactored: subscription.service (3), webhook handler (2), subscription-actions (2). Pattern: find(!isAiPrice) with [0] fallback for period dates.

userStories.title: AI checkout action: trial vs active subscription paths
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want the AI checkout to handle both trial users (Stripe Checkout with billing_cycle_anchor) and active subscribers (subscriptions.update to add item) so that billing is unified.
acceptanceCriteria: createAiCheckoutAction(tier, billingInterval) accepts interval param
Trial path: creates Checkout Session with billing_cycle_anchor = trial end date
Active sub path: calls stripe.subscriptions.update() to add AI item (no redirect)
Returns { url } for checkout path or { immediate: true } for update path
Double-click guard: rejects if AI item already exists on subscription
Unpaid invoice guard: rejects if latest invoice is open/past_due
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Path A (active sub): subscriptions.update + provisioning. Path B (trial): checkout with billing_cycle_anchor. Both guards implemented.

userStories.title: Preview + remove AI add-on actions
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want to preview prorated costs before adding AI and remove the AI add-on with prorated credit so I have full control over my subscription.
acceptanceCriteria: previewAiAddonAction(tier) returns prorated amount via stripe.invoices.createPreview()
removeAiAddonAction() removes AI item via stripe.subscriptionItems.del()
removeAiAddonAction() triggers deprovision after successful removal
Both actions have auth checks and proper error handling
Lint passes
userStories.notes: previewAiAddonAction uses invoices.createPreview. removeAiAddonAction handles single-item (cancel sub) vs multi-item (del item). Deprovision triggered after removal.

userStories.title: Webhook handler: items-based AI detection
userStories.description: As the system, I want the Stripe webhook handler to detect AI items by scanning subscription items instead of metadata so that the single-subscription model works.
acceptanceCriteria: handleSubscriptionCreated/Updated scans items for isAiPrice() matches
AI item found → ensureAiProvisioned (idempotent)
AI item removed → ensureAiDeprovisioned (if was previously active)
Main item handled as before (quantity → lot sync)
Legacy metadata.addon_type fallback preserved for transition
Tier detection uses centralized getTierFromPriceId()
Lint passes
userStories.notes: ensureAiProvisioned (idempotent) + ensureAiDeprovisioned added. Legacy metadata route preserved for pre-unified subs. Items-based detection in both created/updated.

userStories.title: Unit tests: price helpers + action logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests covering the new billing helpers and action logic to ensure correctness before UI work.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests for isAiPrice() and getTierFromPriceId() (positive + negative)
Tests for createAiCheckoutAction trial vs active paths (mocked Stripe)
Tests for removeAiAddonAction (mocked Stripe)
Tests for webhook AI item detection logic
All tests pass with npm test
Lint passes
userStories.notes: 21 new tests added to stripe-utils.test.ts. All 35 passing. Tests cover isAiPrice, isMainPrice, getTierFromPriceId, getIntervalFromAiPriceId, STRIPE_AI_PRICES structure.

userStories.title: Trial auto-conversion: autoConvertTrialWithAi()
userStories.description: As the system, I want the trial expiry cron to auto-add a main per-lot item when a team has AI active + >2 lots, so billing transitions seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: autoConvertTrialWithAi(teamId) in subscription.service.ts
Finds AI Stripe subscription, adds main per-lot item if lotCount > FREE_TIER_LIMIT
No-op if lotCount <= FREE_TIER_LIMIT (free tier + AI allowed)
Updates subscriptions table status: trialing → active (or free_tier)
Trial expiry cron skips teams with active AI sub for standard transition
Sends confirmation email via SubscriptionEmailService
Lint passes
userStories.notes: autoConvertTrialWithAi in subscription.service.ts + cron pre-check for AI trial teams. Email notification on auto-conversion. Cron runs AI check before standard transitions.

userStories.title: UI: billing interval toggle + confirmation modals
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want to choose monthly/annual for AI and see a confirmation modal with prorated amounts before activating, so I understand what I'm paying.
acceptanceCriteria: Trial users see interval toggle (monthly/annual) above tier cards
Trial users see banner with trial end date and auto-conversion notice
Active subscribers: interval auto-detected from existing sub, no toggle
Active subscribers: confirmation modal shows prorated amount from previewAiAddonAction
Active AI: 'Resilier' button with confirmation modal showing prorated credit
All modals have loading states during Stripe API calls
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Interval toggle (month/year) for trial users with -17% badge. Trial banner with auto-conversion notice. Active subs: auto-detected interval, confirmation modal with proration preview from Stripe. Resilier button with AlertDialog. AiSubscriptionInfo extended with subscriptionStatus, trialEnd, billingInterval. Test mocks fixed for price.id.

userStories.title: Settings page: enhanced compact AI card
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want a compact AI card on the settings page that shows my AI status at a glance with contextual CTAs.
acceptanceCriteria: Not active: compact card with price anchor + CTA 'Decouvrir les plans'
Active: status badge, phone number, usage progress bar, conversations count
Active: link 'Gerer' to full AI settings page
Card uses existing settings-page layout patterns
Lint passes
userStories.notes: AiAssistantCard component in settings-page.tsx. Active: green badge, phone, progress bar, conversation count. Inactive: price anchor 49eur + Decouvrir CTA. AI status fetched server-side in parametres/page.tsx via Promise.all with lots.

userStories.title: Dashboard: AI tab always visible + empty state
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want to always see the AI tab in the dashboard (even with 0 conversations) so I can discover the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: AI tab visible in task-type-segment.tsx regardless of count
Non-subscribed: empty state with conversion CTA linking to AI settings
Subscribed: existing AI conversation list behavior
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Removed hide condition from task-type-segment. Added isAiSubscribed prop to OperationsPageClient. Non-subscribed: Bot icon + CTA 'Decouvrir les plans' linking to /gestionnaire/parametres/assistant-ia. AI status fetched in async-operations-content via Promise.all.

userStories.title: Env config + verifyAiCheckoutSession update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want .env.example updated with annual AI prices and verifyAiCheckoutSession updated for single-subscription model.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 new env vars in .env.example: STRIPE_PRICE_AI_*_ANNUAL
verifyAiCheckoutSession finds AI price via isAiPrice() instead of items.data[0]
Legacy AI checkout sessions still handled
Lint passes
userStories.notes: LOW #16 done. aiPriceId extracted via find(isAiPrice) before DB updates.
